events.event_description: George Wadsworth presented credentials as Diplomatic Agent/Consul General, establishing a liaison-level U.S. presence in Lebanon.

evidence.quote: George Wadsworth, Diplomatic Agent/Consul General. Appointed: 1942-10-09. Credentials presented: 1942-11-19. Promoted to Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary 1944-11-16.

events.event_description: George Wadsworth promoted to Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary, elevating the U.S. mission in Lebanon to a legation.

evidence.quote: George Wadsworth, Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary. Appointed: 1944-09-21. Credentials presented: 1944-11-16. Left post on 1947-02-01.

events.event_description: Legation Beirut was raised to Embassy status on August 27, 1952.

evidence.quote: Note: Commissioned during a recess of the Senate. Legation Beirut was raised to Embassy status on August 27, 1952.

events.event_description: All U.S. personnel were withdrawn from Beirut, ending the resident U.S. diplomatic presence in Lebanon.

evidence.quote: After the withdrawal of all U.S. personnel from Beirut on September 6, 1989, McCarthy resided in Washington, D.C.

events.event_description: Ryan Clark Crocker presented credentials as Ambassador, re-establishing the U.S. Embassy in Lebanon after the 1989 withdrawal.

evidence.quote: Ryan Clark Crocker, Ambassador Extraordinary and Plenipotentiary. Appointed: 1990-10-30. Credentials presented: 1990-11-29. Left post on 1993-08-14.
